message.content: what past changes did we tackle and whats remaining

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

---
name: Project Context
description: Crevia MVP — prod URL, stack, launch date, current branch
type: project
originSessionId: 5575c1c2-2ae2-40d7-b4ef-59c8f88d8717
---
Production URL: https://creviamvp.vercel.app
Primary branch: main (deploys directly to Vercel)
Hard launch deadline: 2026-05-04

**Why:** 5-day runway as of 2026-04-29. All remaining work must be scoped to launch-critical only.

**How to apply:** Prioritize unblocking signup (SMTP) and server-side feature gating above all else. Deprioritize anything cosmetic or non-blocking.

Stack: React + TypeScript (Vite), Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage), Vercel, Paystack (payments), Web Crypto API (E2EE). Supabase free tier — rate limits apply (3 confirmation emails/hour).

Completed as of 2026-04-29:
- E2EE key backup v1→v2 security fix (deployed)
- Recovery Password UI (RecoveryPasswordModal, SetRecoveryPasswordDialog) (deployed)
- Auth terms enforcement + improved error messages (deployed)
- handle_new_user trigger uniqueness fix (migration applied to prod)
- Supabase Redirect URLs wildcard added for creviamvp.vercel.app

Blocked as of 2026-04-29:
- Email/password signup returns 500 — Supabase free-tier SMTP rate limit hit (3/hr). Fix: configure Resend SMTP (smtp.resend.com, port 465) or temporarily disable email confirmation
- Recovery password flow not user-tested (blocked by signup being broken)

---
name: Remaining Work Before Launch
description: Feature gaps that must be addressed before the 2026-05-04 hard launch
type: project
originSessionId: 5575c1c2-2ae2-40d7-b4ef-59c8f88d8717
---
As of 2026-04-29, these items are not yet done:

1. **Server-side feature gating** — Client-side limits exist but no server-side RPC guard. A determined user could bypass client checks. Needs a Postgres RPC or edge function that enforces plan limits before writing.

2. **Lead-to-Contract workflow** — Not started.

3. **Bank Integration (USD)** — Not started.

4. **Seat-based pricing enforcement** — Removed from UI; backend never enforced seat limits.

5. **GitHub repo → private** — Settings-only change, no code.

**Completed:** SMTP fixed (Resend SMTP configured in Supabase Auth), WhatsApp chat (grouping, presence, read receipts), 10 premium Crevia Link themes, contract send edge function, footer logo, Pricing page cleanup.

**Why:** Items 1 is highest security risk; 2 and 3 are product features.

**How to apply:** When the user asks what to work on next, surface server-side gating first, then lead-to-contract, then bank integration.

content.text:
Here's a full picture:

---

## Past Changes (What We Tackled)

**Security & Auth**
- E2EE key backup v1 → v2 fix (deployed to prod)
- Recovery Password UI (`RecoveryPasswordModal`, `SetRecoveryPasswordDialog`)
- Auth terms enforcement + improved error messages
- `handle_new_user` trigger uniqueness fix (migration applied to prod)
- Supabase Redirect URLs wildcard for `creviamvp.vercel.app`
- RLS security audit across all tables
- RBAC database policies for workspace members

**Features Shipped**
- WhatsApp-style chat grouping, presence, read receipts (`CreviaChat.tsx`)
- Contract send edge function (email + client notification)
- 36 premium Crevia Link themes
- Verification badge system with admin panel
- Client invoice notifications
- PWA install prompt (landing page)
- PWA install + profile sync + realtime chat reliability fixes
- Footer logo (icon + "Crevia" text, animations)
- Pricing page cleanup (removed Business plan, enforced plan limits in UI)
- Invite links, blank canvas contracts, attachment cards

**Fixes**
- SMTP configured via Resend (unblocked email signup)
- Responsive toolbar on invoice/contract dialogs
- Notification persistence across refreshes
- Error boundary + notification robustness

---

## Remaining Work (What's Left)

| Priority | Item | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **1 — High (security)** | Server-side feature gating (Postgres RPC/edge function to enforce plan limits server-side) | Not done |
| **2 — Product** | Lead-to-Contract workflow | Not started |
| **3 — Product** | Bank Integration (USD) | Not started |
| **4 — Low** | Seat-based pricing enforcement (backend never enforced) | Deferred |
| **5 — Ops** | Make GitHub repo private | Settings-only change |

The launch deadline (May 4) has already passed — do you want to prioritize these or is there something new you're working on?

content: Crevia MVP is live. Remaining critical work is server-side feature gating to prevent plan limit bypasses. Next: implement a Postgres RPC or edge function that enforces plan limits before writes. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text: Let me explore the current codebase to understand what's already in place for leads and contracts.

input.prompt:
Search the codebase at /home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp for anything related to leads, contracts, and the connection between them. I need to understand:
1. Where leads are stored/managed (components, pages, DB tables, types)
2. Where contracts are stored/managed (components, pages, DB tables, types)
3. Any existing "convert lead to contract" or "lead-to-contract" logic (even partial)
4. The Supabase schema files (migrations or types) related to leads and contracts

List relevant file paths and key findings. Be thorough — check src/, supabase/, and any types files.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document by gathering all the relevant information.

## Summary: Leads, Contracts, and Connection Architecture

Based on my thorough search of the Crevia MVP codebase, here are my findings:

### 1. **LEADS TABLE - DOES NOT EXIST**
There is **no leads table** in the Crevia MVP database. After checking:
- All Supabase migrations (47 files)
- The complete Supabase types file
- All source code components
- No "leads" or "CRM" system exists in the codebase

### 2. **CONTRACTS STORAGE & MANAGEMENT**

**Database Table: `contracts`**
- Location: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260106025617_3f4c06f7-3c94-4660-b237-54b9b32b380f.sql`
- Type definition: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` (lines 551-631)

**Schema:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.contracts (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id UUID (references auth.users),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  client_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  client_email TEXT,
  contract_type TEXT ('sponsorship', 'content_creation', 'brand_ambassador', 'ugc', 'affiliate', 'custom'),
  status TEXT ('draft', 'sent', 'signed', 'active', 'completed', 'cancelled'),
  start_date DATE,
  end_date DATE,
  value NUMERIC,
  currency TEXT,
  content TEXT,
  deliverables TEXT[],
  payment_terms TEXT,
  exclusivity BOOLEAN,
  exclusivity_details TEXT,
  usage_rights TEXT,
  termination_clause TEXT,
  creator_signature TEXT,
  creator_signed_at TIMESTAMP,
  client_signature TEXT,
  client_signed_at TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

**Components for Contract Management:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/CreviaContracts.tsx` - Page wrapper
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/ContractsTab.tsx` (644 lines) - Main contracts list, create, filter, search
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/CreateContractDialog.tsx` - Create/edit contracts
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/ContractPreviewDialog.tsx` - View & sign contracts
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/UploadContractDialog.tsx` - Upload document contracts

**Supabase Functions:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/functions/contracts-create/index.ts` - Validates & creates contracts (includes free plan limit: 2/month for free tier)
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/functions/contract-send/index.ts` - Sends contract via email (Resend), creates notifications

**Feature Gating (Free Plan):**
- Location: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260512200000_feature_gating_invoices_contracts.sql`
- Free plan: 2 contracts per month
- Paid plans (creative_pro, brand_workspace): unlimited
- Triggers: `enforce_contract_limit()` on INSERT
- RPC functions: `can_create_contract()` for UI gating

### 3. **CONTRACT-RELATED TABLES (Connected)**

**Chat Messages** (can reference contracts):
- Table: `chat_messages`
- Has column: `contract_id UUID REFERENCES public.contracts(id)`
- Used for contract discussions/collaboration
- File: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260213075441_9347e24e-8235-4523-a885-f02b3a349caf.sql`

### 4. **NO LEAD-TO-CONTRACT CONVERSION LOGIC**
Searching for:
- `lead_to_contract` / `convertLead` / `convert.*lead` - NOT FOUND
- `CRM` / `prospect` / `opportunity` - NOT FOUND
- Leads management components - NOT FOUND
- Leads database table - NOT FOUND

**Conclusion:** There is no existing "convert lead to contract" or any lead management system in the codebase. Contracts are created independently by users.

### 5. **KEY FILES FOR LEADS-TO-CONTRACT IMPLEMENTATION**

If you need to implement this feature, these are the critical files:

**Schema/Migrations:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/` - Add new migration for leads table

**Type Definitions:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` - Update with leads types

**Components to Create:**
- `src/components/studio/LeadsTab.tsx` - Leads list management
- `src/components/studio/CreateLeadDialog.tsx` - Create leads
- `src/components/studio/LeadToContractConverter.tsx` - Convert lead → contract logic
- `src/pages/CreviaLeads.tsx` - Leads page

**Functions to Create:**
- `supabase/functions/leads-create/index.ts`
- `supabase/functions/lead-to-contract-convert/index.ts`

**Related Files (Reference):**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/ContractsTab.tsx` - Model for tab structure
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/functions/contracts-create/index.ts` - Pattern for creating records

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document by gathering all the relevant information.

## Summary: Leads, Contracts, and Connection Architecture

Based on my thorough search of the Crevia MVP codebase, here are my findings:

### 1. **LEADS TABLE - DOES NOT EXIST**
There is **no leads table** in the Crevia MVP database. After checking:
- All Supabase migrations (47 files)
- The complete Supabase types file
- All source code components
- No "leads" or "CRM" system exists in the codebase

### 2. **CONTRACTS STORAGE & MANAGEMENT**

**Database Table: `contracts`**
- Location: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260106025617_3f4c06f7-3c94-4660-b237-54b9b32b380f.sql`
- Type definition: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` (lines 551-631)

**Schema:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.contracts (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id UUID (references auth.users),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  client_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  client_email TEXT,
  contract_type TEXT ('sponsorship', 'content_creation', 'brand_ambassador', 'ugc', 'affiliate', 'custom'),
  status TEXT ('draft', 'sent', 'signed', 'active', 'completed', 'cancelled'),
  start_date DATE,
  end_date DATE,
  value NUMERIC,
  currency TEXT,
  content TEXT,
  deliverables TEXT[],
  payment_terms TEXT,
  exclusivity BOOLEAN,
  exclusivity_details TEXT,
  usage_rights TEXT,
  termination_clause TEXT,
  creator_signature TEXT,
  creator_signed_at TIMESTAMP,
  client_signature TEXT,
  client_signed_at TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

**Components for Contract Management:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/CreviaContracts.tsx` - Page wrapper
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/ContractsTab.tsx` (644 lines) - Main contracts list, create, filter, search
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/CreateContractDialog.tsx` - Create/edit contracts
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/ContractPreviewDialog.tsx` - View & sign contracts
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/UploadContractDialog.tsx` - Upload document contracts

**Supabase Functions:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/functions/contracts-create/index.ts` - Validates & creates contracts (includes free plan limit: 2/month for free tier)
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/functions/contract-send/index.ts` - Sends contract via email (Resend), creates notifications

**Feature Gating (Free Plan):**
- Location: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260512200000_feature_gating_invoices_contracts.sql`
- Free plan: 2 contracts per month
- Paid plans (creative_pro, brand_workspace): unlimited
- Triggers: `enforce_contract_limit()` on INSERT
- RPC functions: `can_create_contract()` for UI gating

### 3. **CONTRACT-RELATED TABLES (Connected)**

**Chat Messages** (can reference contracts):
- Table: `chat_messages`
- Has column: `contract_id UUID REFERENCES public.contracts(id)`
- Used for contract discussions/collaboration
- File: `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260213075441_9347e24e-8235-4523-a885-f02b3a349caf.sql`

### 4. **NO LEAD-TO-CONTRACT CONVERSION LOGIC**
Searching for:
- `lead_to_contract` / `convertLead` / `convert.*lead` - NOT FOUND
- `CRM` / `prospect` / `opportunity` - NOT FOUND
- Leads management components - NOT FOUND
- Leads database table - NOT FOUND

**Conclusion:** There is no existing "convert lead to contract" or any lead management system in the codebase. Contracts are created independently by users.

### 5. **KEY FILES FOR LEADS-TO-CONTRACT IMPLEMENTATION**

If you need to implement this feature, these are the critical files:

**Schema/Migrations:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/` - Add new migration for leads table

**Type Definitions:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` - Update with leads types

**Components to Create:**
- `src/components/studio/LeadsTab.tsx` - Leads list management
- `src/components/studio/CreateLeadDialog.tsx` - Create leads
- `src/components/studio/LeadToContractConverter.tsx` - Convert lead → contract logic
- `src/pages/CreviaLeads.tsx` - Leads page

**Functions to Create:**
- `supabase/functions/leads-create/index.ts`
- `supabase/functions/lead-to-contract-convert/index.ts`

**Related Files (Reference):**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/ContractsTab.tsx` - Model for tab structure
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/functions/contracts-create/index.ts` - Pattern for creating records
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260512200000_feature_gating_invoices_contracts.sql` - Pattern for feature gating

Good — here's the full picture of what needs to be built:

---

## Lead-to-Contract Workflow — What Will Be Tackled

### The Gap
There is **no leads system at all** right now. Contracts exist and work, but there's no way to track a potential deal before it becomes a contract.

---

### What Needs to Be Built

**1. Leads Database Table (new migration)**
Fields: `id`, `user_id`, `client_name`, `client_email`, `client_company`, `deal_value`, `currency`, `status` (`new`, `contacted`, `negotiating`, `won`, `lost`), `notes`, `source`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**2. Leads UI (`LeadsTab.tsx`)**
- List of all leads with status columns (Kanban-style or table view)
- Add new lead form
- Edit/update lead status
- Mark as Won / Lost

**3. "Convert to Contract" Action**
- On a `won` lead, a button pre-fills `CreateContractDialog` with the lead's `client_name`, `client_email`, `deal_value`, `currency`
- Links the resulting contract back to the lead (`lead_id` on the contracts table)
- This is the core of the workflow

**4. Navigation**
- Add a Leads tab alongside Contracts in the Studio/Workspace sidebar

**5. RLS Policies**
- Users can only see/edit their own leads (same pattern as contracts)

---

### What Will NOT Be Built (out of scope)
- Email outreach from within the app
- Lead scoring / automation
- Pipeline analytics (can come later)

---

### Rough Order of Work
1. Migration (leads table + `lead_id` on contracts)
2. Types update
3. `LeadsTab.tsx` + `CreateLeadDialog.tsx`
4. "Convert to Contract" button wired into `CreateContractDialog`
5. Navigation entry

Want to proceed with this plan, or adjust anything (e.g. Kanban vs table view for leads)?
